bird.name: Australian crake
bird.summary: The Australian crake (Porzana fluminea), also known as Australian spotted crake, or spotted crake is a species of bird in the family Rallid…

relations.subject: Australian crake
relations.evidence: It is less common in Tasmania and tropical areas of northern Australia. The Australian crake lives in both coastal and inland habitats in freshwater, brackish, marine and terrestrial environments where it prefers densely vegetated areas of marshes, swamps, estuaries and saltmarshes that support lignum, chenopods, rushes and sedges. Distribution and movement is dependent on water conditions, so following periods of high rainfall, range can extend inland to areas like Alice Springs/Mparntwe
relations.object_summary: 海湾、泻湖、盐沼与河口组成的复合湿地。

relations.object_summary: 淡水与海水交汇形成的高生产力边缘生态系统。

relations.object_summary: 具有较高含水量和密生植被的浅水环境。

relations.subject: Australian crake
relations.evidence: Although the Australian crake is listed as Least Concern, habitat loss, invasive and feral animals, agriculture and livestock grazing and climate change are threats to this species and the conservation of wetlands is fundamental to the survival of this species
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

relations.object_summary: 长期改变水文节律与生态系统稳定性的全球变化因素。
